Used in production by companies such as Shopify and GitLab

No official managed or hosted offering from the Falco project itself, requiring self-hosting

clair.name: Clair
clair.short_description: Clair is an open-source application that statically analyzes container images and reports vulnerabilities affecting their contents.

features.description: Parses image contents to report vulnerabilities without executing the container.
features.title: Static image analysis

features.description: Indexes container image manifests as part of its analysis workflow.
features.title: Manifest indexing

features.description: Matches indexed image contents against continuously ingested vulnerability data.

features.description: Alerts users when new vulnerabilities are found affecting previously indexed images.

features.description: Analyzes images based on Ubuntu, Debian, RHEL, SUSE, Oracle, Alpine, Amazon Linux, VMware Photon, and Python packages.
features.title: Broad distribution support

features.description: Built on the ClairCore library, which separates core scanning logic from the service layer.
features.title: ClairCore library

pros: Free and open source, distributed via GitHub
Static analysis approach avoids running potentially untrusted container images
Broad Linux distribution and package ecosystem support
Notification service keeps previously scanned images current as new vulnerabilities emerge
Used as the vulnerability scanner behind the Quay container registry
cons: Requires self-hosting and operational setup, with no hosted SaaS documented on the project site
No graphical UI is documented on the project's own site; typically consumed via API or an integrated registry like Quay
Documentation on the project homepage is limited compared to some other scanners

faqs.answer: Yes, Clair is open source software available on GitHub.
faqs.question: Is Clair free?

faqs.answer: Clair performs static analysis, parsing image contents rather than executing them, to identify vulnerabilities.
faqs.question: How does Clair analyze images?

faqs.answer: ClairCore is the underlying library that provides Clair's core scanning functionality; Clair itself is a service wrapper around it.
faqs.question: What is ClairCore?

faqs.answer: Yes, Clair includes a notification service that alerts when new vulnerabilities affect already-indexed images.

uberall.name: Uberall
uberall.short_description: Uberall is a multi-location marketing platform for managing listings, reviews, and social presence across search and AI channels.

features.description: Publishes and optimizes business listings across 150+ directories including Google, Apple, and voice search.
features.title: Listings Management

features.description: AI-powered optimization tool for multi-location visibility.
features.title: Geo Studio

features.description: Monitors, responds to, and analyzes customer reviews across all locations.
features.title: Review Management

features.description: SEO-optimized location pages for discovery.
features.title: Local Pages & Locator

features.description: AI-generated social content publishing across Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Google, and Apple.
features.title: Local Social

features.description: An agentic AI product described as continuously monitoring and orchestrating location performance improvements.
features.title: UB-I

pros: Founded in 2013 and headquartered in Berlin, Germany, with a documented track record
Listings sync across 150+ directories including Google, Apple Maps, and voice search
Integrates with Shopify, Salesforce, Zendesk, Tableau, Google BigQuery, Google Ads, and Semrush
Includes an agentic AI product (UB-I) for autonomous location performance monitoring
cons: No public pricing; the three tiers (Show Up, Stand Out, Connect) require requesting a quote
Additional add-ons (Reviews Plus, Analytics Plus, Collaborate Plus, Geo Studio, Social Ads) are priced separately
Company size is not disclosed

faqs.answer: Uberall was founded in 2013 by Florian Hübner and David Federhen.
faqs.question: When was Uberall founded?

faqs.answer: Uberall is headquartered in Berlin, Germany.
faqs.question: Where is Uberall headquartered?

faqs.answer: No, Uberall's three plans (Show Up, Stand Out, Connect) require requesting pricing directly.
faqs.question: Is Uberall pricing public?

faqs.answer: UB-I is described as an agentic AI product that continuously monitors and orchestrates improvements to location performance.
faqs.question: What does UB-I do?

faqs.answer: Uberall publishes and optimizes listings across more than 150 directories, including Google, Apple, and voice search platforms.
faqs.question: Which directories does Uberall publish listings to?

marqii.name: Marqii
marqii.short_description: Marqii manages online listings, reviews, menus, and local SEO for restaurants, used by more than 15,000 locations.

features.description: Automates updates across 70+ platforms including Google, Yelp, Apple Maps, and Facebook.
features.title: Listings Management

features.description: Uses AI to generate review responses automatically.
features.title: Review Management

features.description: Integrates with POS systems to keep menus updated across listings.
features.title: Menu Management

features.description: Creates SEO/GEO-optimized location pages with schema markup.
features.title: Host

features.description: Unifies social media interaction management.
features.title: Social Management

features.description: Builds branded FAQ sections optimized for AI search.
features.title: FAQ Widgets

pros: Used by more than 15,000 restaurants, including named brands like Tacodeli and Insomnia Cookies.
Automates listings updates across 70+ platforms including Google, Yelp, Apple Maps, and Facebook.
AI-powered review responses reduce manual reply workload.
Offers a free AI Visibility Scan before purchase.
cons: No public pricing on the homepage; requires scheduling a demo.
No free trial mentioned, only demos and a free scan.
Founding year and headquarters aren't disclosed on the pages reviewed.

faqs.answer: More than 15,000, including brands like Tacodeli, Insomnia Cookies, Deschutes Brewery, and Cooper's Hawk.

faqs.answer: No trial is mentioned; Marqii offers a free AI Visibility Scan and demo scheduling instead.

faqs.answer: It creates SEO/GEO-optimized location pages with schema markup for each restaurant location.
